name: Chapels and Springs Main Circuit
name_original: Réserve nationale de chasse et faune sauvage d’Arjuzanx - boucle
description: Cycle a moderate 60.5-mile loop around Lake Arjuzanx, enjoying diverse scenery and wildlife in this unique nature reserve.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Mont-De-Marsan traverse the generally flat topography of the Landes department, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by the vast Landes forest, providing shaded routes through pine woods. Rivers such as the Midouze and Adour, along with marshy "barthes," define the landscape, while the Armagnac wine region introduces scenic rides through vineyards. For more varied terrain, the valleys of Chalosse and the hillsides of Armagnac offer gentle ascents. Best road cycling routes around Mont-De-Marsan The most popular road cycling route is Loop from Mont-de-Marsan to Beffroi de Hontanx, a 32.7 miles (52.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular path starting directly from Mont-de-Marsan. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Armagnac Route, a moderate 55.0 miles (88.5 km) path. This route leads through the scenic Armagnac wine region, featuring rolling hills and vineyards. Local road cyclists also love the Loop around Roquefort - Parc Naturel Regional des Landes de Gascogne, a 27.1 miles (43.6 km) trail leading through the regional natural park, often completed in about 1 hour 41 minutes. Road cycling around Mont-De-Marsan is defined by extensive pine forests, river valleys, and the gentle hills of the Armagnac region. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Mont-De-Marsan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 570 reviews. More than 16,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mont-De-Marsan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Mont-De-Marsan?
A: The region around Mont-De-Marsan is primarily characterized by the generally flat topography of the Landes department. You'll find extensive shaded routes through the vast Landes forest, pleasant paths along rivers like the Midouze and Adour, and unique marshy 'barthes'. For those seeking a bit more variation, the Armagnac wine region offers scenic rides through vineyards with gentle rolling hills, and the valleys of Chalosse provide slightly more varied terrain.

Q: Are there many road cycling routes available in the Mont-De-Marsan area?
A: Yes, the Mont-De-Marsan area offers a comprehensive network for road cyclists. There are over 1000 road cycling routes available, catering to various skill levels. This includes approximately 325 easy routes, 637 moderate routes, and 39 more challenging options.

Q: What are some notable natural features to see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes frequently wind through the vast Landes forest, Europe's largest pine forest, offering shaded and scenic environments. You can also explore the banks of the Midouze River and the unique 'barthes' (marshy meadows) along the Adour, which are teeming with birdlife. Further afield, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e829142251/escape-between-lake-sanguinet-biscarosse-and-the-sea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escape between Lake Sanguinet, Biscarosse and the sea&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951631559/landes-de-gascogne-and-great-lakes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landes de Gascogne and Great Lakes Loop&lt;/a&gt; will take you past significant lakes in the wider region.

Q: Are there any dedicated cycle paths or greenways in the region?
A: Absolutely. A major highlight is the &lt;strong&gt;Voie Verte of Marsan and Armagnac&lt;/strong&gt;, a greenway that follows an old railway line for 50 kilometers from Mont-de-Marsan to Gabarret. The section from Villeneuve to Mont-de-Marsan is asphalted and closed to motorized traffic, ensuring a safe and peaceful experience. Mont-de-Marsan is also a key stop on the &lt;strong&gt;EuroVelo 3 (Scandibérique)&lt;/strong&gt;, which offers long-distance cycling through the Armagnac wine region.

Q: What cultural or historical landmarks can I visit while cycling?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover several points of interest. The town of Mont-de-Marsan itself offers cultural attractions like the Musée Despiau Wlérick. For cycling enthusiasts, the &lt;strong&gt;Chapelle Notre Dame des Cyclistes&lt;/strong&gt; in Labastide d'Armagnac is a must-visit, housing a collection of cycling jerseys and historic bicycles. You might also encounter local heritage signs along the Voie Verte of Marsan and Armagnac, telling stories about local customs. The historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/676194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Labastide-d'Armagnac&lt;/a&gt; is another notable landmark.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes near Mont-De-Marsan?
A: Yes, the region's generally flat topography and dedicated greenways make it suitable for family cycling. Routes like the Voie Verte of Marsan and Armagnac are closed to motorized traffic, providing a safe environment. For an easy, shorter option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956668004/loop-around-roquefort-parc-naturel-regional-des-landes-de-gascogne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop around Roquefort - Parc Naturel Regional des Landes de Gascogne&lt;/a&gt;, which is 43.6 km long and leads through the regional natural park.

Q: What do other road cyclists think about the routes in Mont-De-Marsan?
A: The road cycling routes around Mont-De-Marsan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 570 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet woodlands, the well-maintained paths, and the diverse scenery ranging from pine forests to vineyards.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes starting from Mont-De-Marsan?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956853935/loop-from-mont-de-marsan-to-beffroi-de-hontanx" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop from Mont-de-Marsan to Beffroi de Hontanx&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 52.6 km trail that starts directly from the town. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964388127/mezos-and-mimizan-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mézos and Mimizan Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 33.3 km route that explores the coastal area.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Armagnac wine region?
A: Definitely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938918602/armagnac-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Armagnac Route&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 88.5 km path specifically designed to lead you through the scenic Armagnac wine region, featuring rolling hills and vineyards. The EuroVelo 3 also traverses this area, offering a chance to experience local heritage and viticulture.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Mont-De-Marsan?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. The vast Landes forest provides shade, making summer rides more comfortable. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for exploring the vineyards and riverbanks. Winter can be cooler but still offers opportunities for cycling on less crowded paths.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options available?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, Mont-De-Marsan is a significant stop on the &lt;strong&gt;EuroVelo 3 (Scandibérique)&lt;/strong&gt;, a long-distance route extending from Norway to Spain. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938739485/haute-landes-and-pays-de-born-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haute-Landes and Pays de Born Circuit&lt;/a&gt; (107.1 km) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951631559/landes-de-gascogne-and-great-lakes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landes de Gascogne and Great Lakes Loop&lt;/a&gt; (136 km) offer substantial distances for experienced cyclists.

Q: Where can I find information about public transport or parking for cyclists in Mont-De-Marsan?
A: Mont-de-Marsan is well-connected, and information regarding public transport options and designated parking areas for cyclists can typically be found on the official tourism website for the region. For specific details on local transport and parking facilities, you may consult the Mont-de-Marsan Tourism Office website: &lt;a href="https://en.montdemarsan-tourisme.com/mont-de-marsan-la-parenthese-inattendue-en/balades-randonnees-en/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;montdemarsan-tourisme.com&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: A pleasant view of Lake Arjuzanx. There are also a number of picnic benches to stop for a break.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to road cycle the National hunting and wildlife reserve of Arjuzanx - loop?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling loop, covering approximately 97.4 km, generally takes around 3 hours and 49 minutes to complete. This duration is for continuous cycling and doesn't include stops for breaks or sightseeing.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved surfaces, making it ideal for road cycling. While the reserve offers diverse trails, this specific loop is designed to be smooth and suitable for racebikes.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this 97.4 km loop with about 400m of elevation gain requires a good level of fitness. While some routes in the Arjuzanx area are easy, this particular loop might be challenging for absolute beginners due to its distance and duration.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the National hunting and wildlife reserve of Arjuzanx - loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact loop aren't detailed, the Arjuzanx reserve generally offers parking facilities near popular access points around Lake Arjuzanx. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient starting points.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and picturesque scenery, including views of Lake Arjuzanx, surrounded by forests, wetlands, and moors. The reserve is also a significant ecological habitat, particularly known as a strategic site for migratory Common Cranes, offering excellent opportunities for wildlife observation.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle the Arjuzanx loop?
faqs.answer: The reserve is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn are often ideal for cycling due to milder temperatures and the chance to observe migratory birds like the Common Cranes. Summer can be pleasant, but be prepared for warmer weather.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers views of Lake Arjuzanx. You'll also pass by the Rion-des-Landes Church, which is a highlight along the way.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers about 33% of the 'Chapelles et sources (Circuit principal)' and smaller sections of 'Circuit de la forêt, la chapelle de Suzan, les sources guérisseuses et le chemin de halage de la Midouze' and 'Circuit VTT des chemins de halage de l'Adour et de la Midouze'.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the National hunting and wildlife reserve of Arjuzanx - loop?
faqs.answer: As this route is within the National Hunting and Wildlife Reserve of Arjuzanx, specific regulations for dogs may apply to protect wildlife. It's best to check the official reserve guidelines regarding pets, especially leash rules, before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Réserve Naturelle d'Arjuzanx?
faqs.answer: The Arjuzanx National Nature Reserve, officially classified in September 2022, generally does not require an entrance fee for public access to its trails. However, for specific activities or guided tours, it's always a good idea to check the official reserve website for any updated regulations or permit requirements.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the reserve?
faqs.answer: While the reserve itself is focused on nature, the surrounding towns and villages, such as Arjuzanx and Rion-des-Landes, offer options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation. These can be found a short distance from the cycling loop.
